On this page

  • Kaplan-Meier: 90-Day Survival by HRS Reversal
  • Logistic Regression: 90-Day Mortality
  • Fine-Gray Competing Risk: 90-Day Mortality (Death vs. Transplant)

Example

Author

Tianqi Ouyang

Published

April 1, 2026

Kaplan-Meier: 90-Day Survival by HRS Reversal

Code 
fit <- survfit(
  Surv(time_to_death_90days, time_to_death_status_90days) ~ HRS_reversal_new,
  data = final_master
)

ggsurvplot(
  fit,
  data = final_master,
  surv.scale = "percent",
  pval = TRUE,
  pval.coord = c(75, 1),
  pval.size = 4,
  conf.int = FALSE,
  xlim = c(0, 90),
  xlab = "Time (days)",
  break.time.by = 30,
  tables.theme = theme_cleantable(),
  risk.table = TRUE,
  risk.table.y.text.col = TRUE,
  risk.table.y.text = TRUE,
  risk.table.fontsize = 4
)

Logistic Regression: 90-Day Mortality

Multivariate logistic regression with HRS reversal as primary exposure, adjusted for age, race, ethnicity, MELD 3.0, and transplant listing status.

Code 
model_logistic <- glm(
  time_to_death_status_90days ~
    HRS_reversal_new + age + race_2 + hispanic + admit_meld_3 + listed_transplant,
  family = binomial,
  data = final_master
)

tbl_regression(model_logistic, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
  add_n() %>%
  bold_p(0.05) %>%
  bold_labels()
Characteristic N OR1 95% CI1 p-value
HRS_reversal_new 243


    0
— —
    1
0.72 0.39, 1.32 0.3
age 243 1.03 1.00, 1.06 0.030
race_2 243


    0
— —
    1
1.03 0.47, 2.32 >0.9
hispanic 243


    0
— —
    1
0.87 0.38, 1.92 0.7
admit_meld_3 243 1.04 1.00, 1.09 0.042
listed_transplant 243


    0
— —
    1
0.22 0.11, 0.40 <0.001
1 OR = Odds Ratio, CI = Confidence Interval

Fine-Gray Competing Risk: 90-Day Mortality (Death vs. Transplant)

Multivariate Fine-Gray competing risk model. Death (failcode = 1) is the event of interest; transplant (failcode = 2) is the competing event; cencode = 0 is censored.

Code 
model_fg <- tidycmprsk::crr(
  Surv(time_to_death_90days_cmp, time_to_death_status_90days_cmp) ~
    HRS_reversal_new + age + race_2 + hispanic + admit_meld_3,
  failcode = 1,
  cencode = 0,
  data = final_master
)

tbl_regression(model_fg, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
  add_n() %>%
  bold_p(0.05) %>%
  bold_labels()
Characteristic N HR1 95% CI1 p-value
HRS_reversal_new 243


    0
— —
    1
0.71 0.45, 1.12 0.14
age 243 1.01 0.99, 1.04 0.2
race_2 243


    0
— —
    1
1.13 0.65, 1.97 0.7
hispanic 243


    0
— —
    1
0.79 0.45, 1.40 0.4
admit_meld_3 243 1.03 1.00, 1.06 0.059
1 HR = Hazard Ratio, CI = Confidence Interval
